scope_note: QA Rank of Apparition = Unified Prime Splitting Formula cert [416]. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.2307/2309169 (rank of apparition alpha(p): smallest n>=1 with F_n≡0 mod p; alpha(p)|p-1 for split, alpha(p)|p+1 for inert); Lucas, E. (1878) 'Theorie des fonctions numeriques simplement periodiques' American Journal of Mathematics 1(2) pp.184-240 (original Fibonacci periodicity theory; Lucas sequences, periodicity conditions, apparition theory for primes); Lehmer, D.H. (1930) 'An extended theory of Lucas functions' Annals of Mathematics 31(3) doi:10.2307/1968235 (rank of apparition in general Lucas sequences; divisibility properties alpha(p)|pi(p) and entry quotient pi(p)/alpha(p) in {1,2,4}). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): The rank of apparition alpha(p) — the smallest n>=1 with F_n ≡ 0 mod p, computed by QA T-step iteration T(b,e)=(e,b+e) in pure integer arithmetic (Theorem NT) — unifies the two-condition prime splitting criterion of cert [415] into a SINGLE formula: alpha(p) | p - (5/p), where (5/p) is the Kronecker symbol: (5/p)=+1 for split (p%5 in {1,4}), (5/p)=-1 for inert (p%5 in {2,3}), (5/p)=0 for ramified p=5. The Kronecker symbol is computed by pure integer arithmetic from p%5. (C1) UNIFIED FORMULA: alpha(p)|p-(5/p) verified for all 45 primes tested (22 split <=491, 22 inert <=193, p=5 ramified): split target=p-1, inert target=p+1, ramified target=p=5 with alpha(5)=5; falsifiable: any prime where alpha(p) does not divide p-(5/p) refutes. (C2) ALPHA DIVIDES PISANO: alpha(p)|pi(p) for all 45 primes; standard result (Wall 1960, Lehmer 1930); the rank always divides the Pisano period. (C3) ENTRY QUOTIENT: pi(p)/alpha(p) in {1,2,4} for all 45 primes; secondary structure — inert primes with alpha=(p+1)/2 give quotient 4 (pi=2(p+1)), those with alpha=p+1 give quotient 2; split primes vary. (C4) QA ORBIT READING: alpha(p) is the step index at which the QA T-orbit of (F_0,F_1)=(0,1) mod p first returns a zero first-component (F_alpha≡0 mod p); verified for sample primes p in {11,41,3,7,5}; Kronecker symbol then determines the divisibility class. CHAIN: cert [133] T-step sign-flip f(e,b+e)=-f(b,e) -> cert [414] f(a,b)=a^2+ab-b^2=N_{Q(sqrt5)/Q}(a+b*phi) -> cert [415] pi(p)=T-orbit period encodes prime splitting (two separate conditions) -> cert [416] alpha(p)|p-(5/p) unifies to one formula. The unification is: cert [415] shows pi(p)|p-1 or pi(p)|2(p+1); cert [416] sharpens to alpha(p)|p-1 or alpha(p)|p+1 (or alpha(p)|p for ramified) with a single expression. The Kronecker symbol (5/p) is the quadratic character of 5 mod p — it equals the splitting type of p in Q(sqrt5)/Q, the same number field whose norm form equals the QA Eisenstein form (cert [414]). The chain thus closes: Q(sqrt5) appears at the algebraic level (norm form), at the Galois level (splitting type via Kronecker), and at the dynamical level (T-orbit period mod p). All integer. Derived 2026-06-14.
